signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Positioning
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.51, hitting a 6-week low, amid renewed intervention fears as the pair breaks above the 160.00 threshold. The 287bp SOFR-TONA carry premium continues to support USD/JPY, while JPY COT LF net short −105,136 (week ending Jun 2) indicates a crowded spec short. The options market shows a moderate put skew with 25d RR at -1.19, suggesting hedging against JPY strength despite the spot move. Intervention risk is acute on sustained moves above 160.00, especially with the BoJ's next meeting on 16 Jun. Watch for official comments or a sharp reversal.

signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + CESI + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7005, with AUD as the weakest G10 currency this week (avg -1.263% vs peers) and USD as the strongest (avg +0.816%). The 2.079% leader-to-laggard spread highlights significant divergence. AUD's CESI net -8 confirms data disappointment, while the -75bp SOFR-AONIA carry premium still favors AUD. However, the strong USD bid from hot US inflation and Fed hawkish bets is overriding carry. Watch for a shift in US inflation data or RBA's next meeting on 16 Jun to alleviate AUD pressure.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5798. NZD COT LF net short −27,797 (week ending Jun 2) indicates specs are heavily short, while the RBNZ has a 'Hold' bias with a mildly accommodative real rate of -0.83%. Retail sentiment shows an extreme 90% long bias, diverging from institutional positioning. This setup creates short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ signals a hawkish shift or if the market re-evaluates the 'least respected' Kiwi. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) is on 8 Jul.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Premium + Carry
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.7987. IV at 20.6% vs HV30 at 6.6% results in a +14.0% VRP, indicating an extreme elevated volatility premium and strong hedging demand for USD upside protection. This is significantly above realized volatility. The 375bp Fed-SNB carry premium strongly favors USD, reinforcing the bid. The elevated VRP suggests options traders are paying up for protection against further CHF weakness, despite the neutral 25d RR at +0.14. Watch for any shift in risk sentiment or SNB commentary at their next meeting on 18 Jun.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Decision + Positioning + Vol
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1550, gathering strength ahead of today's ECB rate decision (11 Jun). The pair trades with a 167bp SOFR-€STR carry disadvantage for EUR. EUR COT LF net short −22,320 (week ending Jun 2) indicates speculative short positioning. IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.3% shows a +2.8% VRP, suggesting elevated hedging demand for EUR downside protection, despite the current bid. The 25d RR at -0.63 shows a mild put skew. The market is hedging against potential dovish surprises from the ECB, even with a 'Hike' bias priced in OIS.
